# Modelplane Documentation

> Modelplane is the open source control plane for AI model serving.
> It extends Crossplane to manage AI inference across a fleet of GPU clusters.
> Source: https://modelplane.ai/

## Pages

- Overview: https://modelplane.ai/
- Getting Started: https://modelplane.ai/getting-started/
- Concepts: https://modelplane.ai/concepts/
- API Reference: https://modelplane.ai/reference/

## Overview

Modelplane draws a clear boundary between two teams.

**Platform teams** create `InferenceClusters` describing their GPU fleet and `InferenceClasses` defining hardware recipes (GPU type, count). They set organizational metadata via labels: tier, region, provider.

**ML teams** create a `ModelDeployment` carrying everything needed to serve a model: the worker template, hardware topology, and replica count. Modelplane schedules each replica to a ready cluster with matching capacity, composes a `ModelReplica` per cluster, and creates `ModelEndpoints` for routing. A `ModelService` routes traffic across endpoints through a unified Envoy Gateway endpoint on the control plane.

Modelplane is the fleet-level control plane above the inference engine.

## Quick-start example

```yaml
apiVersion: modelplane.ai/v1alpha1
kind: ModelDeployment
metadata:
  name: qwen-demo
  namespace: ml-team
spec:
  replicas: 2
  workers:
    topology:
      tensor: 1
    template:
      spec:
        containers:
        - name: engine
          image: vllm/vllm-openai:v0.23.0
          args:
          - "--model=Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct"
```

This deploys two replicas of Qwen 2.5 0.5B and produces a unified, OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

## API types

### InferenceCluster
Represents a GPU cluster available for scheduling. Platform teams register clusters here with capacity information and labels (tier, region, provider).

### InferenceClass
Defines a hardware recipe — GPU type, count, and configuration. Platform teams create these; ML teams reference them in ModelDeployments.

### ModelDeployment
The primary unit of model serving. Carries the worker container image, hardware topology, and replica count. Modelplane's scheduler matches it to InferenceClusters.

### ModelReplica
One instance of a model on a specific cluster. Composed by Modelplane — not created directly by users.

### ModelEndpoint
A routing endpoint for a ModelReplica on a specific cluster. Also composed by Modelplane.

### ModelService
Aggregates all ModelEndpoints for a ModelDeployment into a single, unified, OpenAI-compatible Envoy Gateway endpoint on the control plane.

## Current status (v0.1)

- Cluster sources: GKE (provisioned), Existing (bring-your-own kubeconfig)
- Serving engines: vLLM
- Scaling: `spec.replicas` on ModelDeployment
- Routing: Unified OpenAI endpoint via ModelService + Envoy Gateway

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/modelplaneai/modelplane
- Issues: https://github.com/modelplaneai/modelplane/issues
- Discussions: https://github.com/modelplaneai/modelplane/discussions
